objectives: Define eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a square matrix
Derive the characteristic equation det(A - λI) = 0
Compute eigenvalues and eigenvectors for a 2×2 matrix through a worked example
Explain the geometric interpretation of eigenvectors as invariant directions
State the spectral theorem for symmetric matrices

required_claims.statement: A non-zero vector v is an eigenvector of matrix A if Av = λv for some scalar λ (the eigenvalue)
Eigenvalues are roots of the characteristic polynomial det(A - λI) = 0
A real symmetric matrix has real eigenvalues and orthogonal eigenvectors
